observations: Skeletonization: scikit-image skeletonize works (0.014s for 512x512 mask)
Graph built via: pure-Python fallback (sknw blocked by numba/llvmlite build)
Connected components: 139 (graph fragmentation — naive tracer needs refinement)
Largest component: 2 nodes — pure-Python tracing algorithm produces fragmented topology
Node attributes: y, x, pts available and geo-annotatable
Geo coordinate annotation: 236/236 nodes annotated (pixel → lat/lon)
NetworkX algorithm compatibility: betweenness centrality computed (0.015s)
Core concept validated: skeleton → graph conversion → NetworkX algorithms works end-to-end
notes: sknw not installable: depends on numba which depends on llvmlite; llvmlite build requires system LLVM and fails with uv/pip on this macOS setup
Pure-Python fallback validates technical feasibility but produces fragmented graph topology — a proper implementation (closely following sknw's algorithm) would resolve this
No fundamental architectural flaw detected; the skeleton→graph pipeline is valid but needs a working library or a better pure-Python implementation
This is a known challenge; sknw exists specifically to solve this exact problem
follow_up: Sprint 0: resolve numba/llvmlite build for sknw, or evaluate alternative skeleton-to-graph packages (skeleton2graph, custom implementation)
The architectural assumption (mask → skeleton → graph) is valid. The tooling choice (sknw) needs environment resolution.
